name: json_tool
description: Parse, validate, query, transform, and format JSON data. Supports dot-notation path queries (e.g., 'users.0.name'), deep merge, structural diff, key enumeration, and in-place set/delete operations. All operations return structured results that can be chained into further tool calls.

action.description: Operation to perform. parse: parse JSON string into object. validate: check JSON syntax. query: extract value at a path. format: pretty-print or minify. merge: deep-merge two objects. diff: compare two objects. keys: list all key paths. set: set a value at a path. delete: remove a key.

input.description: JSON string to operate on. Required for all actions. Alternative: use 'file' to point to a JSON file.

input_b.description: Second JSON string for merge and diff actions. Alternative: use 'file_b'.

path.description: Dot-notation path to a value within the JSON (e.g., 'users.0.name', 'config.timeout'). Required for query, set, delete. Optional for keys (scopes the key listing).

value.description: JSON-encoded value to set at the given path (for 'set' action). Must be valid JSON: e.g., '"text"', '42', 'true', '[1,2,3]', '{"key":"val"}'.

indent.description: Indentation spaces for 'format' action. 0 produces minified single-line JSON. Defaults to 2.

recursive.description: For 'keys' action: include keys from nested objects and arrays. Defaults to true.

file.description: Path to a JSON file to use as the primary input. Alternative to 'input'.

file_b.description: Path to a second JSON file for merge/diff actions. Alternative to 'input_b'.
